svg.text: Legal & Compliance View of the Architecture -- Workforce Activity Insights Platform
Non-Technical Overview for Legal, Privacy, Risk & Audit Stakeholders
* Understand workforce activity trends across teams
* Operational visibility for manager productivity insights
* Internal enterprise use on company-managed devices
KEY: Measures activity signals (interaction counts), not activity content (what was typed/read/viewed)
Designed for aggregated team-level trends -- not personal surveillance or content monitoring
A small company-installed app on employee Windows/Mac devices
* Runs quietly in the background on company-managed devices
* Records only limited activity signals for work pattern analysis
* Deployed and controlled by company IT (MDM tools)
* Can be signed with a company certificate for authenticity
* Updates managed via standard enterprise deployment
Agent = a small application installed on a company device
Limited work activity indicators -- not the actual content of work
Data NOT Collected -- Excluded by Design
Data is protected while being sent from devices to company systems
[LOCK] TLS / HTTPS -- All data encrypted while traveling over the network.
Like a sealed envelope -- even if intercepted, contents cannot be read.
[SHIELD] Rate Limiting -- Prevents abuse or flooding (120 requests/min limit).
[CHECK] Trusted Routes Only -- Only approved devices and paths can send data.
Unauthorized senders are rejected before reaching the application.
[VERIFY] Input Validation -- Every signal is checked for correct format and ranges.
it is authorized before sending data.
* Tokens expire and can be revoked
Managers log in using the company's
Company servers receive, validate, and summarize activity signals
1. APIs receive data -- secure endpoints accept signals
2. Validation checks -- format and range verification
5. Read-time processing -- raw data stored as-is
API = a secure communication point used by software to send/receive data
Technical: "Role-Based Access Control with Team Hierarchy"
People only see the information they are allowed to see
[USER] Managers can only see their own team's summarized data
[USER] Employees can only see their own personal activity summary
[TEAM] Team isolation -- one team cannot see another team's data
[LOCK] Server-enforced -- access controlled on server, not by user links
[LOG] Cross-scope attempts -- blocked and recorded in audit log
Authorization = rules about what data each person can access, based on their job role
Company-controlled systems with access restrictions and retention rules
[LOCK] Encryption at rest -- stored data protected from unauthorized access
[LOCK] Row-level security -- database restricts which rows each query sees
[CLOCK] Automatic retention -- old data deleted per configured policy
Retention: Configurable (default 14 days, up to 90 days per policy).
Activity data auto-purged. Audit logs retained indefinitely for compliance.
Important actions are recorded for accountability and review
* Rate limit violations and system events
[LINK] Each action tagged with: who, IP, when, unique request ID
Audit trail = a permanent history of important actions for accountability
Managers see summarized patterns and trends, not raw content.
Reporting is aggregate and operational -- not personal surveillance or content review.
Built to minimize data from the start.
See only what your role requires.
All safeguards enforced server-side -- cannot be bypassed by users, dashboards, or client changes.
1. The system captures activity metadata (interaction counts, app names, idle time), not content (text, screenshots, URLs, messages, files).
2. Access is role-limited and team-restricted. Managers see only their own team's aggregated data.
3. Sensitive content types (typed text, screenshots, emails, personal files) are architecturally excluded from collection.
4. Reporting is primarily aggregate and operational -- designed for team-level work pattern visibility.
5. Data retention and access controls should be configured to align with company policy and jurisdictional requirements.
6. Final deployment should remain subject to review and approval by Legal, Privacy, HR, and Information Security teams.
Summary -- What This Means in Simple Terms
[OK] The project helps the company understand work activity trends while limiting personal data collection.
[OK] It uses secure login, encrypted storage, restricted access, and comprehensive audit records.
[OK] Designed for controlled internal operational visibility -- not intrusive content monitoring or surveillance.
[OK] All controls are server-enforced, audited, and testable -- do not rely on user behavior or client-side settings.
43+ automated security tests verify access controls, team isolation, and authentication enforcement.
System Data Flow -- End to End
Audit log (immutable record of all actions)
Content (typed text, screenshots, URLs, messages, files) never enters this system.
Only interaction counts, app names, and idle time are processed.
Agent -- Small app on a device
Encryption -- Scrambles data so unauthorized people cannot read it
Authorization -- Rules deciding who can access what data
Audit Trail -- Permanent record of important actions for accountability
Retention -- How long data is kept before automatic deletion
Row-Level Security -- Database restricts which rows each query can see
OIDC -- Industry standard for secure company login (used by Azure AD, Okta)
For internal legal/compliance review -- terminology simplified for non-technical stakeholders
Zinnia Axion -- Workforce Activity Insights Platform -- Architecture Document v1.0 -- March 2026
